skill.name: animejs
skill.description: Anime.js adapter patterns for HyperFrames. Use when writing Anime.js animations or timelines inside HyperFrames compositions, registering animations on window.__hfAnime, making Anime.js seek-driven and deterministic, or translating Anime.js examples into render-safe HyperFrames HTML.
skill.summary: Anime.js animation patterns for HyperFrames video compositions with seek-driven timing control.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: Security audit complete. All 28 static findings evaluated as false positives. The skill is legitimate Anime.js documentation for HyperFrames integration. External command flags are Markdown code block delimiters. Network flags are standard CDN URLs for the Anime.js library. No malicious content detected.

content.user_title: Add Anime.js Animations to HyperFrames Videos
content.value_statement: Authors need to add smooth JavaScript animations to video compositions. This skill provides patterns for integrating Anime.js with HyperFrames seek-driven rendering.
actual_capabilities: Create Anime.js animations that work with HyperFrames seek engine
Register animations on window.__hfAnime registry for automatic seeking
Build Anime.js timelines with sequential animation sequences
Use ES module imports for Anime.js library
Validate compositions with hyperframes lint and validate commands
Translate existing Anime.js examples into render-safe code
limitations: Does not support autoplay animations (must use autoplay: false)
Infinite loops are not supported (must use finite repeat counts)
Animations built in timers, promises, or event handlers will not seek correctly
GSAP is recommended for complex scene sequencing

use_cases.title: Add simple DOM animations to videos
use_cases.description: Use Anime.js to animate text, buttons, and UI elements with smooth easing curves.

use_cases.title: Convert existing Anime.js examples
use_cases.description: Translate Anime.js code snippets into HyperFrames-compatible seek-driven animations.

use_cases.title: Create micro-animation sequences
use_cases.description: Build multiple independent animations registered in window.__hfAnime for synchronized playback.

prompt_templates.title: Basic animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Add an Anime.js animation to element .mark that moves it 280px to the right with easeOutExpo easing.

prompt_templates.title: Timeline sequence
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Anime.js timeline where .title fades in first, then .accent scales in after 250ms.

prompt_templates.title: ES module setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up Anime.js using ES module import from CDN and animate .chip elements.

prompt_templates.title: Validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Lint and validate a composition that uses Anime.js animations for the registry pattern.

output_examples.input: Add an animation to element .mark that moves right 280px
output_examples.output: Anime.js animation registered on window.__hfAnime with autoplay: false, translateX: 280, and easeOutExpo easing

output_examples.input: Create a timeline with title fading in, then accent scaling
output_examples.output: Anime.js timeline with .title fadeIn first, then .accent scale after 250ms delay
Run hyperframes lint and hyperframes validate commands to check animation registry usage

best_practices: Always set autoplay: false so HyperFrames controls the animation timing
Register every animation on window.__hfAnime for automatic seek support
Use finite durations and repeat counts based on composition length
anti_patterns: Do not leave autoplay at default (true) - animation will not seek correctly
Do not use infinite loops - compute finite repeat count from composition duration
Do not build animations in timers, promises, or event handlers

faq.question: Why does autoplay need to be false?
faq.answer: HyperFrames owns the clock. Setting autoplay: false lets HyperFrames seek animations deterministically at any timestamp.

faq.question: How do I register animations for seeking?
faq.answer: Push animations to window.__hfAnime array: window.__hfAnime = window.__hfAnime || []; window.__hfAnime.push(anim);

faq.question: Can I use infinite loops with Anime.js?
faq.answer: No. Use finite repeat counts. Calculate repeat count from your composition duration divided by animation duration.

faq.question: GSAP or Anime.js - which should I use?
faq.answer: GSAP is the primary HyperFrames authoring path for complex sequences. Use Anime.js for simple SVG and DOM flourishes.

faq.question: How do I validate my Anime.js composition?
faq.answer: Run npx hyperframes lint and npx hyperframes validate after editing to check for registry and autoplay issues.

faq.question: Can I use Anime.js with ES modules?
faq.answer: Yes. Import from https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/animejs/+esm. The adapter only needs objects with seek(), pause(), and play() methods.
